brief_justification: Paper A is a normative meta-science argument urging conventions for evaluating statistical multiverse analyses (e.g. judging a multiverse by its single worst universe) to curb abdication of responsibility and doubt-manufacturing; Paper B is a rigorous quantum-information proof that additivity of minimum output p-Renyi entropy fails, built from correlated random-projection channel constructions. There is no transferable shared machinery — no common governing equation, process, or estimation principle — only a faint, generic 'minimum/worst-case' resemblance, which is not shared mechanism.
